description: Phase 2A — Foundation for the /generate router. Ship the router skill plus two Pandoc-based handlers (book, pdf), the artifacts/ convention, and provenance frontmatter. See vaults/llm-wiki/wiki/concepts/output-skills-roadmap.md for context.

userStories.title: Scaffold /generate router skill
userStories.description: As a user, I want a /generate command that mirrors /ingest — dispatching to per-artifact-type handler skills — so that artifact generation follows the established router pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .claude/skills/generate/SKILL.md with proper YAML frontmatter (name, description, argument-hint)
Skill accepts first positional arg as artifact type (book, pdf, slides, etc.)
Skill looks up a handler skill named generate-<type> and invokes it with remaining args
Errors clearly when no handler is registered for the requested type, listing known handlers
Accepts --vault flag to target a specific vault (default: resolve from cwd like /ingest does)
SKILL.md explains the mirror-of-/ingest mental model and lists currently-implemented handlers
userStories.notes: Handler discovery should be convention-based — look for .claude/skills/generate-*/SKILL.md. No registry needed.

userStories.title: Define artifacts/ directory convention and provenance frontmatter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a predictable location and metadata contract for generated artifacts so that drift detection and close-the-loop testing work in later phases.
acceptanceCriteria: Decide on storage path: vaults/<vault>/artifacts/<handler>/<topic>-<date>.<ext>

Each artifact gets a .meta.yaml sidecar with: generated-from (wiki page paths), source-hash (sha256 of concatenated source pages), generator (handler name + version), generated-at (ISO date), template (template name used)
Update the vault template (.claude/skills/vault-create/) to gitignore artifacts/ by default
Document the convention in sites/docs/src/content/docs/reference/ as a new page 'Artifact conventions'
Add a tiny shared helper (bash or python) that computes source-hash given a list of wiki paths so each handler reuses it
Update vaults/llm-wiki/wiki/concepts/output-skills-roadmap.md and close-the-loop-testing.md cross-refs if the paths change
userStories.notes: Keep the sidecar tiny — handlers must be able to read/write it without a YAML library if possible.

userStories.title: Implement generate-book handler
userStories.description: As a user, I want `/generate book <topic>` to produce a PDF book from wiki pages matching the topic, with a table of contents and cross-references preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .claude/skills/generate-book/SKILL.md
Handler accepts a topic (tag, folder path, or 'all') and optional --vault flag
Handler selects matching wiki pages, concatenates them with headings preserved, runs Pandoc
Output written to vaults/<vault>/artifacts/book/<topic>-<date>.pdf plus .meta.yaml sidecar
Lazy-installs pandoc via brew if missing; prints install instructions if brew unavailable
Handles wikilinks: converts [[page]] to in-PDF internal links or footnotes
Includes title page derived from topic; auto-generated table of contents; preserves mermaid diagrams (via mermaid-cli fallback or static image)
Skill documents: template location, how to customise the LaTeX/HTML template, how Pandoc errors surface to the user
userStories.notes: Mermaid is nice-to-have for 2A; if brittle, punt to 2B and ship without mermaid rendering first. — Done: mermaid punted to 2B (renders as fenced code block); wikilinks render as italic inline text; LaTeX fallback to HTML when no engine installed.

userStories.title: Implement generate-pdf handler
userStories.description: As a user, I want `/generate pdf <page-or-folder>` to produce a shareable PDF from a specific wiki page or sub-section, without the book ceremony (TOC, title page).
acceptanceCriteria: Create .claude/skills/generate-pdf/SKILL.md
Handler accepts a page path or folder path (relative to wiki/) and optional --vault
Output written to vaults/<vault>/artifacts/pdf/<slug>-<date>.pdf with .meta.yaml sidecar
Uses Pandoc with a minimal print stylesheet — no title page, no TOC unless --toc passed
Shares the same install / hash / template machinery as generate-book (DRY — factor common helpers)
Handles multi-page folder input by concatenating pages in filename order
userStories.notes: Factor shared Pandoc invocation into a helper to avoid copy-paste between book and pdf handlers. — Done: extracted ensure-pandoc.sh + render-pdf.sh under .claude/skills/generate/lib/; generate-book refactored to call them, generate-pdf built on top from the start.

userStories.title: Document Phase 2A in sites/docs
userStories.description: As a new user, I want usage docs for /generate, generate-book, generate-pdf, and the artifacts/ convention so I can actually use Phase 2A.
acceptanceCriteria: Add sites/docs/src/content/docs/features/generate.md (top-level overview of /generate + mirror-of-ingest framing)
Add sites/docs/src/content/docs/features/generate-book.md with usage, example command, expected output, customisation
Add sites/docs/src/content/docs/features/generate-pdf.md with usage and examples
Add sites/docs/src/content/docs/reference/artifacts.md documenting the storage path, sidecar schema, source-hash algorithm
Update sites/docs/src/content/docs/reference/commands.md with new /generate command row
Cross-link from the features index page
All new pages include a mermaid diagram where a flow is described (per project doc-standards)
userStories.notes: This is the final story for the phase — nothing to build, just write. Should be fast if earlier stories landed cleanly. — Done: features/generate.md + generate-book.md + generate-pdf.md added, commands.md updated with /generate row + handler table + internal-skill rows, overview.md updated with artifact-generation section, reference/artifacts.md written in US-002. Observatory-themed mermaid on every flow-description page.
